DISPATCHER REPORTS MULTIPLE REPORTS OF DOWNED TREES, POWER LINES, AND POWER OUTAGES ACROSS DICKINSON COUNTY. WE ENERGIES OUTAGE MAP INDICATING OUTAGES STRETCHING FROM THE FELCH AREA DOWN TO THE WI BORDER.

QUARTER SIZED HAIL REPORTED A FEW MILES SOUTHWEST OF CHASSELL IN RURAL HOUGHTON COUNTY.
REPORT FROM SOCIAL MEDIA. PICTURES SHARED OF DOWNED TREE BRANCH AND A FLIPPED CAR PORT. OBSERVER SAID A LOT OF ACCUMULATING PEA SIZED HAIL AND EXTREMELY HEAVY RAIN. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

A MICROBURST HIT THE INDIAN RIVER CAMPGROUND WEDNESDAY AFTERNOON. OVER 50 TREES OVER A 300 YARD X 200 YARD AREA WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 85 MPH. FORTUNATELY, ALL OF THE CAMPSITES WERE EMPTY AT THE TIME OF THE STORM.
CAMPGROUND HOST OF INDIAN RIVER CAMPGROUND REPORTS 400-500 FOOT AREA OF TREES UPROOTED, MANY 3 FEET OR MORE IN DIAMETER. THE CAMPGROUND IS INACCESSIBLE. NO INJURIES REPORTED.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT WITH ESTIMATED PRECIPITATION OF AT LEAST 7 INCHES BASED FROM PHOTO SUBMISSION. RADAR ESTIMATES AROUND 4 TO 6 INCHES OF PRECIPITATION. TIME ESTIMATED USING RADAR.
PUBLIC IMAGE ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF HERMANSVILLE GOLF COURSE WITH STANDING WATER ON COURSE. RADAR ESTIMATES AROUND 4 TO 6 INCHES OF PRECIPITATION. TIME ESTIMATED USING RADAR.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT WITH ESTIMATED PRECIP AMT OF 8 INCHES. RADAR DUAL POL ESTIMATES RANGE FROM 5 TO 7 INCHES WITH LEGACY ESTIMATED AMOUNTS RANGING FROM 7 TO 9 INCHES. ROAD FLOODED.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORT OF 58MPH WIND GUST. TIME ESTIMATED WITH DUAL-POL RADAR.
IRONWOOD PUBLIC SAFETY DEPARTMENT REPORTS EXTENSIVE STORM DAMAGE THROUGHOUT GOGEBIC COUNTY WITH NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

U.S. NATIONAL FOREST SERVICE RANGERS FROM THE OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST REPORTED SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE ALONG FOREST SERVICE ROAD 9300 AND WOLF MOUNTAIN ROAD IN THE OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST. WIND GUSTS IN MARENISCO WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 90MPH DURING THIS EVENT. DELAYED REPORT.

U.S. NATIONAL FOREST SERVICE RANGERS FROM THE OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST REPORTED SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE ALONG THE GREAT LAKES ROAD APPROX. 5 MILES NORTHWEST OF MARENISCO IN THE OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST. WIND GUSTS IN MARENISCO WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 90MPH DURING THIS EVENT. DELAYED REPORT.

U.S. NATIONAL FOREST SERVICE RANGERS REPORTED DOWNED AND UPROOTED TREES AT THE ACCESS ROAD INTO THE OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST BOBCAT LAKE CAMPGROUND LOCATED TO THE SOUTHEAST OF MARENSICO. FOREST SERVICE PERSONNEL CLEARED TREES AS CAMPERS WERE TEMPORARILY STRANDED AT THE CAMPGROUND. DELAYED REPORT.

U.S. NATIONAL FOREST SERVICE RANGERS FROM THE OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST REPORTED DOWNED AND UPROOTED TREES ALONG U.S. FOREST SERVICE ROAD 8190 OR DUNHAM ROAD LOCATED WEST OF MARENISCO. U.S. FOREST SERVICE PERSONNEL CLEARED TREES AND REGRADED THE ROAD AS THE ROAD WAS TEMPORARILY BLOCKED BY FALLEN TREES. WIND GUSTS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 90MPH IN MARENISCO DURING THIS EVENT. DELAYED REPORT.

MARENISCO TOWNSHIP FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE AT A NEWLY BUILT GARAGE STRUCTURE AT THE CORNER OF HALL AND MILL STREETS IN MARENISCO. OVER A QUARTER OF THE METAL ROOF AND DECKING WAS STRIPPED BACK AND TORN FROM THE ROOF TRUSSES. DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED ON THE SECTION OF THE ROOF ABOVE THE GARAGE DOOR. ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS IN MARENISCO AT THIS TIME WAS UP TO 90MPH. DELAYED REPORT.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TREES DOWN, SEVERAL ROAD CLOSURES, AND NUMEROUS POWER OUTAGES NEAR MARENISCO. TIME ESTIMATED WITH RADAR.

A PRELIMINARY DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE IN THE TOWN OF MARENISCO WITH ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS UP TO 90 MPH. SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS FOUND, BUT MOST DAMAGE WAS UPROOTED OR SNAPPED TREES. A CONTINUOUS SWATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE STARTED APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES WEST OF TOWN TO 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF TOWN AND CONTINUED INTO THE OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST. CAMPERS WERE TRAPPED AT BOBCAT LAKE CAMPGROUND DUE TO FALLEN TREES, BUT NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. CLEAN UP CONTINUES AND THE USFS IS PLANNING TO COORDINATE A FLY OVER NEXT WEEK. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

PART OF PICTURE HAS DEAD TREE BLOWN DOWN. DAMAGE BEGUN AROUND 1130-1145 CDT ACCORDING TO REPORT/RADAR. FROM TWITTER. DELAYED REPORT. LOCATION OF REPORT IS PETERSON DRIVE.
